title: Building Setu: Democratizing Legal Aid in Nepal with AI
excerpt: How we designed and built Setu, an AI-powered legal assistant that drafts Nepali legal documents and analyzes texts for inherent bias.
content:
# Building Setu: Democratizing Legal Aid in Nepal with AI

Legal aid is often expensive and hard to navigate, especially in Nepal where many official procedures require structured legal Nepalese documents. Setu was built to bridge this gap.

## The Concept

Setu uses Large Language Models and natural language processing to:
- Automate legal document drafting based on user prompts.
- Scan legal documents to highlight discrepancies, missing clauses, or potential biases.

## AI in Nepal's Legal System

Adapting modern NLP models to local context and terminology was a significant engineering challenge. We focused on localizing dataset ingestion and translation verification so that documents are legally accurate and linguistically correct in Nepali.
author: Rupak Adhikari

title: Getting Started with ROS2 and Gazebo for Robotics Simulation
excerpt: A beginner's guide to setting up Robot Operating System 2 (ROS2) for autonomous navigation and sensor fusion mapping.
content:
# Getting Started with ROS2 and Gazebo

Robotics engineering is shifting rapidly towards modular software frameworks. ROS2 (Robot Operating System) provides a standard middleware layer to manage sensor messages, actuator nodes, and hardware control.

## Why ROS2?

Unlike ROS1, ROS2 is built with production standards, real-time control, and multi-robot systems in mind. Pairing it with Gazebo allows robust simulation of LiDAR sensors, IMU drift, and wheel encoders before deployment on hardware.
author: Rupak Adhikari
